links.targetTitle: PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.
PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.
GSOC provides many-to-one notifications with sub-second latency via symmetric shared-key SOCs.
Bee Reactor Adapter requires full Bee node, mutable stamp, wallet, and open P2P ports

global.title: Chat requires both users to run full Bee nodes because light nodes cannot receive PSS or GSOC.
global.content:
## Hard requirement

The Swarm chat/collab feature requires **both users to run full Bee nodes**. This is not a soft recommendation — light nodes lack the neighborhood-sync capability needed to receive PSS Trojan chunks and GSOC SOC updates.

## Capability matrix

| Feature | Full Node | Light Node | Ultra-Light |
|---------|-----------|------------|-------------|
| PSS send | Yes | Yes | No |
| PSS receive | **Yes** | No | No |
| GSOC subscribe | **Yes** | No | No |
| GSOC send | Yes | Yes | No |
| Feed read/write | Yes | Yes | Read only |

## Minimum viable Bee config

- `full-node: true`
- `swap-enable: true`
- A funded **mutable** postage stamp (GSOC requires mutable)
- Connected to Gnosis Chain RPC

## Dev mode caveat

PSS and GSOC do function in `bee dev` mode, but only within the same node — there are no peers. Multi-node testing requires either a real Swarm network or a local multi-node setup.
global.description: PSS receive and GSOC subscribe both require full-node capability. Light and ultra-light nodes can send PSS/GSOC but cannot subscribe to incoming messages.
